rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROLINE MARGARET MEHEGAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/29/20. Prior to review, a filed-stamped copy of the amended petition and supporting declaration must be provided at least 2 weeks prior to the continued hearing date. The Notice of Hearing on filing of the amended petition must also be re-served as 1) the notice indicates that an "amendment" has been filed, rather than a fully amended petition; and 2) notice of filing a pleading cannot be served prior to the filing of the pleading.

rulings.Case Title: CONS. OF NANCY CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/26/2020 per counsel's request (via e-mail 06/22/2020). *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SYLVIA MAUREEN HAYMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Terminate Formal Proceedings; And For Authority To Administer Estate Pursuant To Probate Code Section 7660(A)(1)
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner requests termination of formal probate proceeding, as estate was valued at less than $150,000 at the time of death. 2) Joyce Mason, one of the two beneficiaries, was provided notice of the appointment petition, but Petitioner now alleges that she is deceased, and neither the date of death nor her successors are known at this time. No request to dispense notice has been made.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DELORES OLLISON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner requests admission of lost will. Petitioner alleges that Decedent did not have a copy of the will and was not competent at time of death as she was conserved. Petitioner does not discuss level of competency required to revoke a will. 2) Proposed executor, Sherrie Guillory, does not have a phone number. 3) The testamentary instrument lodged with the court not only contained the will but also two trust instruments: an "Agreement with Respect to Status of Real and Personal Property" and Summary of Certification". It is unclear whether additional action must be taken by Petitioner prior to Will being admitted.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIA LUCRECIA ABREGO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/21/20. No courtesy copies of the amended petition and subsequently filed papers have been received yet by the assigned Examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.93 G.] A petition cannot seek to both admit a will and/or to have Letters of Administration issued. No proof of holographic instrument has been filed. Courtesy copies must be received by 7/9/20 to avoid another continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIA P CORPUS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/29/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) A renunciation signed by Doris Corpus as a nominated co-executor is not on file. See LRSF 14.33. 2) Notice of the petition to administer the estate must be served on the alternate co-executors named in the will. See Probate Code § 8110(b). A supplemental proof of service must be filed. 3) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) The date of death of the decedent's spouse must be specified. See LRSF 14.26(E)(3). B) Paragraph 8 of the petition must list the name, relationship, age, and address of each alternate co-executor named in the will. *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.
